key_insight: A single high-resolution magneto-optical image of magnetic domain texture can be decoded to infer the underlying magnetic field, temperature, and magnetic history.
engineering_relevance: This enables single-shot, image-based multiparameter sensing for magnetic materials and supports data-driven analysis of complex magnetic states.
method: Collected pixel-resolved optical polarization maps of feature-rich magnetic domains in a bismuth-substituted yttrium iron garnet film.
Trained deep convolutional neural networks to regress external conditions from the magnetic texture.
Compared deep learning with feature-based neural inference using hand-crafted, physically interpretable image descriptors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a controlled inference experiment that reconstructs field, temperature, and history from a single fine-scale magnetic texture image, showing accurate multiparameter decoding.
watch_item: The mapping is nonlinear and ambiguous due to stochastic nucleation and hysteresis, so performance may depend strongly on material system, image quality, and measurement conditions.
